---
name: style-enforcer
description: "Two-stage .NET style enforcement workflow for C# and project config files (analyze first, then propose fixes)."
---

# Style Enforcer Skill (.NET)

## Use When


Use this skill when the user asks to:
- Review C# code quality/style in this workspace
- Enforce workspace style rules consistently
- Propose safe refactors and targeted fixes
- Audit .NET project/config files for correctness/security

## Inputs

- target: required file path or pasted snippet
- applyFixes: optional (false by default). If omitted, only propose fixes.

## Stage 1 - Analyze (No edits)

Review target using all active workspace instructions and scoped instructions.

Return findings in this exact structure:

Rule 1 (4-Space Indent): PASS/FAIL with line-specific reason  
Rule 2 (const and typing): PASS/FAIL with line-specific reason  
Rule 3 (Document Methods): PASS/FAIL with line-specific reason  
Rule 4 (Keep Methods Small): PASS/FAIL with line-specific reason  
Rule 5 (Avoid dynamic): PASS/FAIL with line-specific reason

Suggested Fixes:
- Concrete edits with line references
- Prioritize highest-impact fixes first

Highest-Impact Issue: one sentence  
Quick Win: one fix that can be applied in under 5 minutes

Additional requirements:
- If target is config, print: Config file type detected: <type> before findings.
- If a secret/credential risk exists, list it first.
- Never skip a rule. If uncertain, mark FAIL and explain.

## Stage 2 - Propose Fix Patch (No auto-apply by default)

After analysis, produce:
1. Minimal edit plan (ordered)
2. Unified diff patch for the target file
3. Post-fix expected rule status (PASS/FAIL)

Safety rules:
- Preserve runtime behavior unless change is required by a rule.
- Do not perform broad/unsafe refactors without explicit user approval.
- Do not auto-apply edits unless user explicitly requests apply.

## If User Asks to Apply Fixes

Only then:
- Apply the minimal safe patch
- Re-run the rule checklist
- Return final PASS/FAIL and residual risks

## Scope Notes

- Primary: **/*.cs
- Also supports: **/*.csproj, **/Directory.Build.props, **/Directory.Build.targets, **/global.json, **/appsettings*.json
- Respect exceptions for generated code markers (<auto-generated/>, @ignore-style)
